## The Code: Multi-Head Attention in Python

```{.python include="src/python/common.py" snippet="mha_params"}
```

Each attention head is a triple of weight matrices $(W_q, W_k, W_v)$, each of shape $[d \times d_k]$.
`MultiHeadAttention` groups the heads with the output projection $W_o$.
`make_multi_head_attention` checks that the model width can be split evenly, allocates one parameter set per head, and creates the final output projection.

```{.python include="src/python/common.py" snippet="mha_forward"}
```

`multi_head_attention` runs each head's SDPA independently, concatenates the per-head outputs column-wise, and applies the output projection.

```{.python include="src/python/chapter_demos.py" snippet="chapter_08"}
```

The demo creates a two-head attention module, runs it over a four-token sequence, and reports the output shape plus the number of attention-weight matrices.
Run it with `python3 src/python/chapter_demos.py`.
